title: Grastidium cyanopterum
taxonomy: Grastidium cyanopterum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Restionaceae, which is commonly known as the restio or club-rush family. The family Restionaceae is part of the larger order Poales, which includes various other plant families such as the grasses and sedges. Restionaceae is a small family of plants that are mostly found in southern hemisphere regions. They are typically found in coastal areas and are often associated with salt spray and salt-tolerant vegetation. Grastidium cyanopterum is native to Australia.
